title: inula candida subsp. limonella
intro: Inula candida subsp. limonella, also known as the Limonella Inula, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), characterized by its small yellow or white flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Inula candida subsp. limonella include relief from digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, and its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried herb, tea, or tincture, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
